who_paragraphs: To Andrew's Corner είναι ένας χώρος φιλοξενίας και κοινότητας στη Θεσσαλονίκη. (Παρακαλούμε εισάγετε τη δική σας μετάφραση εδώ).
why_paragraphs: Οραματιζόμαστε μια γειτονιά όπου οι άνθρωποι συνδέονται ουσιαστικά. (Παρακαλούμε εισάγετε τη δική σας μετάφραση εδώ).
institute_paragraphs: (Παρακαλούμε εισάγετε τη δική σας μετάφραση εδώ).

who_paragraphs: Andrew's Corner is a library, café, and spiritual resource center in the heart of Thessaloniki. We're a low-tech, hi-relation, face-to-face community.

Our graduate level library is non-circulating but available for everyone in person. Our 30,000+ volumes are especially strong in philosophical and biblical resources from which one may explore the roots of European culture.

Among us you will find lively discussions about human life and values, God, Jesus' Way, and the big questions of our age. Philosophy nights, book discussion groups, strength-training and community BBQs – not to mention great coffee! – are just a few features of this vibrant community. You're welcome here too!
why_paragraphs: The Apostle Andrew encapsulates our purpose and vision. Andrew appears three times in John's gospel. Each time, he introduces someone directly to Jesus. Each time, following the introduction, Andrew fades from view and the story follows how Jesus interacts with the individual introduced. Jesus also connects to each person a bit differently.

In Andrew's third and final appearance, he introduces some Greeks who are seeking Jesus (John 12:20ff). So, before Paul brings the Christian faith to Greece, Andrew introduces a few Greeks directly to Jesus. This meeting is so important, in John's gospel, that it is the only occasion in which God the Father speaks out loud to the world (12:28). Meeting Jesus means becoming acquainted with the whole Trinity.

Our hunch, at Andrew's Corner, is that Jesus still interacts directly with people today. By our name and activities, we quietly point people to Jesus and then fade into the background. Jesus guides each person as he sees fit.
en.institute_title: Our Institute for Scripture, Philosophy, and Culture
institute_paragraphs: Andrew's Corner also houses our Institute for Scripture, Philosophy, and Culture. St. Andrew's Institute provides an avenue for scholarly exploration of the deep thought structures of European culture that are embedded in the seminal texts of the Hebrew Bible, the Greek Septuagint and New Testament, and the corpus of Greek and Roman philosophy. We host working groups, public lectures and seminars, as well as offer select accredited courses in cooperation with Aristotle University of Thessaloniki.
